question: Compare the NuScale reactor safety valve (RSV) design philosophy with conventional PWR safety valves.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications, and highlights corrosion protection measures such as weld cladding with stainless steel and nickel-based alloys.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet NRC-endorsed conditions as specified in relevant regulatory guides and federal regulations. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, tested, and inspected to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, and classified according to NRC quality group requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and form part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself serving as the steam generator shell and pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how these components are integrated within the module, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary systems, and references other sections for specific design bases and descriptions.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat from the reactor after shutdown, ensuring safe cooling without external power during non-LOCA events. It maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours, preventing fuel or pressure boundary limits from being exceeded, and its reliability and risk significance are systematically evaluated. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing validates the new support design, and the most likely degradation mechanism remains wear, with secondary corrosion risks considered low and manageable within standard plugging criteria.

edges.description: Section A describes the physical components and materials of the NuScale pressurizer, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME, 10 CFR) that govern the design and testing of reactor coolant boundary components, including safety valves, thus connecting the design details in A to the compliance requirements in B.

Section A describes the materials and components of the NuScale pressurizer, referencing Section 5.2 for material details, while Section B provides the regulatory and industry standards (such as EPRI guidelines and ASME NQA-1) that govern the integrity and material selection for the reactor coolant boundary, including those components described in Section A.

Section A discusses the regulatory codes and standards (such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that the NuScale RSV design must comply with, while Section B lists specific references (including ASME and EPRI documents) that provide the technical basis and guidance for those codes and standards, thus Section B supports the claims of compliance made in Section A.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, which is part of the reactor coolant boundary discussed in Section A.

Section A establishes that the NuScale reactor safety valve design complies with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III, while Section B elaborates on overpressure protection provisions and further compliance with specific code cases and inservice testing requirements under the same regulatory framework, thus both sections are connected by their reference to regulatory compliance for safety valve design and operation.

Section A describes compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for reactor coolant boundary components, while Section B lists references including ASME and EPRI documents that support or inform those compliance statements.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system components, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B references specific sections that detail the materials and components of the pressurizer, which is structurally related to the RSVs as part of the integrated system.

Section A identifies the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as integral components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the design and operation of such components, including RSVs, must satisfy.

Section A describes the components included in the NuScale reactor design, specifically mentioning reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and quality assurance of reactor coolant system components, which would include the RSVs.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system components, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B references specific material details and locations for pressurizer components, some of which are physically and functionally related to the RSVs within the integrated design.

Section A describes the specific components and design philosophy of the NuScale reactor safety valve (RSV) within the reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements (such as ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that the RSV design must satisfy.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B lists references that provide standards and guidelines (such as ASME NQA-1 and EPRI documents) which underpin the design and integrity requirements for these components.

Section B provides references to material specifications and component descriptions that are structurally related to the pressurizer, which is a key component involved in the safety valve design discussed in Section A's context of decay heat removal.

Section A describes the design basis and function of the NuScale Decay Heat Removal System, which ensures safe shutdown by removing decay heat, while Section B outlines the overpressure protection requirements and code compliance for the reactor coolant boundary, both of which are connected through their roles in ensuring reactor safety under abnormal conditions.

Section A describes the design basis and function of the NuScale Decay Heat Removal System, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines relevant to reactor coolant boundary integrity, indicating that Section A's design philosophy may be informed by or aligned with the standards cited in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system design, while Section B specifies detailed material and component information for the pressurizer, which is a subsystem mentioned in Section A.

Section A outlines the unique NuScale RCS design philosophy (natural circulation, no external piping), which directly informs the overpressure protection approach described in Section B, where compliance with ASME and NRC codes for safety valves is specified.

Section A describes the design basis and unique features of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B lists references that provide supporting standards and guidelines relevant to the integrity and quality assurance of reactor coolant boundary components, connecting Section A's design philosophy to established industry practices.

Section A discusses the general design and degradation risks of reactor coolant system components, while Section B provides specific material details for the pressurizer, with both sections referencing component design details from the same FSAR chapter, thus Section B specifies material information that supports the broader component design context in Section A.

Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the NuScale reactor safety valve design must comply with, which serve as the requirements that the design philosophy in Section A must satisfy.

Section A discusses the design and operational experience of reactor coolant system piping, referencing industry guidelines and standards, some of which are listed in Section B as formal references supporting the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary.

Section A and Section B are identical excerpts describing the components contained within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, including the reactor safety valves (RSVs), thus providing the same foundational information for comparing design philosophies.

Section A lists the decay heat removal system (DHRS) as one of the integrated components within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, while Section B describes the design basis and function of the DHRS, thus Section A provides a general overview that Section B elaborates on in detail.

Section A lists the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as integral components within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, while Section B describes the overall design basis of the reactor coolant system, which includes the RPV and its integral components such as the RSVs, thus Section A specifies details about components that are generally described in Section B.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor, including the placement of RSVs within the reactor pressure vessel alongside steam generators, while Section B discusses the operational experience and design considerations of steam generators in conventional PWRs, providing a basis for comparing how RSVs are integrated and protected in both designs.

Section A identifies the reactor safety valves (RSVs) as integral components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B describes the decay heat removal system (DHRS) that relies on these components, indicating that the RSVs' design supports the DHRS function for safe shutdown.

Section A details the specific components and subsystems of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the RSVs, while Section B provides the overall design basis and operational philosophy (such as reliance on natural circulation) that underpins the selection and integration of these components.

Section A outlines the integrated design of the NuScale reactor, including the placement and function of reactor safety valves (RSVs), while Section B discusses the operational experience and design considerations of steam generator (SG) tubing in conventional PWRs, providing a basis for comparing the safety valve design philosophies by highlighting differences in component integration and risk management.

Section A describes the Decay Heat Removal System's design basis for removing residual and decay heat, while Section B provides the overall design basis and configuration of the reactor coolant system, which is the system that the DHRS operates within and cools.

Section A outlines the design basis and function of the NuScale Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) for post-trip cooling, while Section B discusses the integrity and risk profile of steam generator (SG) tubing in the reactor coolant system, with both sections connected through their focus on maintaining safe reactor cooling and system integrity under various conditions.

Section A describes the overall design philosophy of the NuScale reactor coolant system, emphasizing natural circulation and the absence of external piping, while Section B compares specific component risks (such as steam generator tube wear) in NuScale to those in conventional PWRs, connecting both sections through their discussion of design differences and safety considerations.
